title: Half Adder Logic Circuit
examTip: Sum is A XOR B, Carry is A AND B. Remember that a Half Adder cannot accept a carry-in from a previous column, which is why it is called a 'half' adder.
theory:
A Half Adder is a fundamental combinational digital logic circuit that performs the addition of two single-bit binary numbers, commonly labeled A and B. It produces two outputs: Sum (S) and Carry (C).

1. Sum (S) output: The Sum is calculated using a two-input Exclusive-OR (XOR) gate. The boolean equation is S = A ⊕ B. S is high (1) only when one of the inputs A or B is high, but not both.

2. Carry (C) output: The Carry is calculated using a two-input AND gate. The boolean equation is C = A · B. C is high (1) only when both inputs A and B are high (1).

In binary addition, the operations are: 0 + 0 = Sum 0 Carry 0; 0 + 1 = Sum 1 Carry 0; 1 + 0 = Sum 1 Carry 0; 1 + 1 = Sum 0 Carry 1. The limitation of the half adder is that it can only add two single bits. It cannot handle an incoming carry-in bit (Cin) from a previous stage of addition. For multi-bit addition, a Full Adder must be used.
keyPoints: Half Adder adds two single-bit inputs A and B
Produces two outputs: Sum (S) and Carry (C)
Sum logic uses an XOR gate: S = A ⊕ B
Carry logic uses an AND gate: C = A · B
Cannot handle an incoming carry-in (Cin) bit
useCases: Used in the least significant bit (LSB) position of multi-bit adders
Fundamental building block for ALU (Arithmetic Logic Unit) designs
Address calculation in processors and memory units
